meta.title: RelayPulse - Real-time availability matrix for API relay services
meta.description: RelayPulse - Real-time monitoring of LLM relay services worldwide for availability, latency, and sponsored routes, helping developers quickly evaluate provider quality and discover the most stable API providers. Supports connectivity checks for mainstream models such as Claude and GPT.

dataReference.title: Real Consumption Probing
dataReference.text: Probes are initiated based on actual token usage; results vary with network conditions and provider load, faithfully reflecting API response quality

neutralMaintenance.title: Open and Transparent Operations
neutralMaintenance.text: Entire codebase is open source under MIT license, self-hosting available for verification. Sponsors receive pinned display, exclusive tags, and high-frequency monitoring, but availability data remains objective and accurate

monitoringScope.title: Full-Stack Protocol Verification
monitoringScope.text: Covers TCP connectivity, RTT latency, and deep validation of response content, effectively detecting "fake alive" APIs that connect but cannot converse

liability.title: Disclaimer
liability.text: Monitoring data is for technical reference only and does not constitute endorsement of any third-party provider's credibility or financial security. Users interact with providers at their own risk

agreement.title: Listing Terms & Acknowledgement
agreement.intro: This is a paid listing. Please read and check each item; all must be confirmed before submitting:
agreement.clausePaid: I understand listing is paid (sponsorship-based; pulse reference price ¥100/channel/month, prepaid monthly). Lapsed renewals are auto-downgraded and moved to the secondary board, with no refund of fees already paid.
agreement.clauseApiKey: I authorize the platform to use the provided API Key for config validation, availability monitoring and in-depth quality detection (including response metadata, SSE timing, cache tokens, model fields and self-reported identity parsing, covering diag.relaypulse.top) and related display; listing is not possible without this consent.
agreement.clauseQuality: I warrant that the channel's actual model quality matches the declared source (an official declaration must be a genuine official API); if inconsistent, the platform may add a negative annotation, delist, or move it to the cold board.
agreement.clauseLegit: I hold lawful rights to use the provided endpoint, API Key, brand and links, and the submitted information is truthful, accurate and compliant.
agreement.clauseNoEndorse: I understand sponsorship badges and pinning are display/monitoring benefits only and do not constitute the platform's endorsement of reputation, funds or service quality; the platform may unilaterally delist for compliance risk or complaints.
agreement.allRequiredHint: Please check all items before submitting

meta.title: Relay Station Detection - Test if an API relay is degraded or swapping models | RelayPulse
meta.description: RelayPulse relay detection: monitor relay availability in real time and use blind fingerprint forensics to spot model masquerading, fake streaming, and silent downgrades. See each relay's real quality score with traceable evidence.

hero.h1: Relay Station Detection: is your API relay the real thing, or watered down?
hero.lead: Many people use cheap API relays to reach Claude and GPT, but paying for opus doesn't guarantee you get opus. RelayPulse gives you two layers of objective signal — availability monitoring plus blind quality fingerprinting — so you can judge each relay yourself, with traceable evidence instead of the vendor's word.

what.title: What relay detection is, and why it matters
what.intro: An API relay forwards your request to an upstream model, usually cheaper than going direct. But cheap can hide four kinds of problems that vendor marketing won't show:

items.d: Dropped connections, timeouts, rate limits — you paid but can't use it, or it works only intermittently.
You asked for opus; behind the scenes it quietly serves cheaper haiku or an unknown model.
It looks like token-by-token streaming, but it's buffered and dumped at once — sometimes truncated mid-way.
Knowledge cutoff and behavioral fingerprint no longer match the official model — the upstream was swapped.

how.title: How RelayPulse detects: two layers of signal
how.intro: We don't decide who is a fraud for you. We provide two kinds of objective, reproducible signal, and let you judge:

availability.d: Every minute we probe each channel's connectivity, latency, and rate limiting, rendered as a timeline heatmap. This is RelayPulse's home turf — one of the earliest relay availability monitors.

quality.d: Without telling the model who it is, we hit the official baseline and each relay with the same probes, then compare response fingerprints: digit-count volume for tier (haiku / sonnet / opus), knowledge-cutoff probes, reasoning volume, and streaming granularity. The further a fingerprint drifts from the baseline, the less likely it is the model it claims to be.

how.note: The method is public and reproducible — that's the core difference between us and navigation sites that rank by ad spend.

ranking.title: Live quality ranking
ranking.intro: Below is each relay's current fingerprint quality score (0–100, higher is closer to the official baseline), sorted high to low. Sponsorship does not affect the order.
ranking.disclaimer: This is a fingerprint quality score — it measures how closely a relay matches the official model, not overall performance. For latency and success rate, see each relay's detail page. Click Evidence for the per-run record.
ranking.empty: No quality data yet (rpdiag integration disabled or no blind-test results).

tactics.title: Common watering-down tactics
tactics.intro: Knowing these tactics helps you read what's behind a quality score. We describe the mechanism only, without naming names — for which relay and which run, see the per-run evidence on each channel's detail page:

items.name: Model masquerade / swap
items.how: Routing opus or sonnet requests to a cheaper model. How to check: give the model hard reasoning and digit-heavy tasks, then compare fingerprint volume against the official baseline.

items.name: Fake streaming
items.how: Not real streaming: buffered then dumped, or truncated mid-way. How to check: inspect whether chunk timing and granularity match the official model.

items.name: Silent downgrade
items.how: Swapping a high-tier model for a low-tier one at peak hours, then back at off-peak. How to check: long-running blind tests, watching whether the score swings on a schedule.

items.name: Backend drift
items.how: A changed upstream provider shifts knowledge cutoff and behavior. How to check: knowledge-cutoff probes plus identity / behavior fingerprint comparison.

tactics.evidenceNote: The concrete measured evidence for each category is available run-by-run on the matching channel's detail page — public and reproducible.

diy.title: How to check a relay yourself
steps: Read the availability timeline: is it stable, are there recurring drops or rate limits?
Read the fingerprint quality score: does it look like the official model, is the score persistently low?
Read the per-run evidence: open the detail page and see which dimension each blind test diverged on.
diy.cta: Submit a relay for blind testing
diy.ctaDesc: Want to test a relay we haven't listed yet? Submit it and we'll add it to the blind-test queue.

items.q: How can you tell a relay is watered down?
items.a: Look at two layers: the availability timeline (is it stable) and the fingerprint quality score (does it match the official model). A score persistently below the official baseline, or one that swings on a schedule, often signals a downgrade or model swap; the detail page shows the per-run evidence.

items.q: Does a low quality score always mean it's fake?
items.a: Not necessarily. A low score can come from an unstable upstream, rate limiting, or a genuine downgrade. We only give an objective fingerprint-similarity score and never declare real vs fake — the evidence is public, and the conclusion is yours to draw.

Check three things: is the method public and reproducible, is the data traceable, and is the ranking influenced by ad money. Sites that give verdicts without evidence, or rank by referral fees, are of limited value.

items.q: How do I check relay availability?
items.a: RelayPulse probes each channel's connectivity, latency, and rate limiting every minute, rendered as a timeline heatmap, with history over 90 minutes / 24 hours / 7 days / 30 days.

items.q: How are you different from navigation sites and leaderboards?
items.a: RelayPulse runs on provider sponsorship, but sponsorship does not affect detection results or quality ranking; our probing method and data are public and reproducible. We are a neutral quality-and-availability dashboard, not a referral directory.
